ASBESTOS MOTION CALENDAR ON FEB-24-2005 IN 206, PLAINTIFF'S MTN FOR PREFERENCE IN SETTING CASE FOR TRIAL PURSUANT TO CCP 36(A) & (D) WAS PARTLY GRANTED AND PARTLY DENIED. THE COURT GRANTED PREFERENCE PURSUANT TO CCP 36(A) ONLY. THE TRIAL DATE IS SET FOR 6/13/05 @ 11:15AM IN DEPT. 206; SETTLEMENT CONFERENCE SET FOR 6/3/05 @ 9:30AM IN DEPT. 304. DISCOVERY IS OPEN UNTIL THE CASE IS ASSIGNED TO A TRIAL COURT. PARTIES STIPULATE TO G.O. 140 RE: MOTIONS FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT. PLAINTIFF SHALL DISCLOSE ALL PRODUCT IDENTIFICATION AND CAUSATION WITNESSES AS TO EACH DEFENDANT BY 4/15/05, AND SHALL ALSO PRODUCE DOCUMENTS THEY INTEND TO USE AT TRIAL AS TO PRODUCT IDENTIFICATION AS TO EACH DEFENDANT BY THAT DATE. IF PLAINTIFF LEARNS OF NEW WITNESSES, IN GOOD FAITH, THEY SHALL IMMEDIATELY BE DISCLOSED. PLAINTIFF REPRESENTED THAT THIS CASE IS AT-ISSUE. THE TRIAL SETTING IS CONDITIONAL UPON THE DEPOSITION OF PLAINTIFF BE COMPLETED BY 3/30/05. AUTHORIZATION TO BERRY AND BERRY BY 3/3/05. ORDER SIGNED. HON. ROBERT DONDERO; CLERKS: V. MU/D. OKAZAKI REPORTER: JOANN BRYCE, #3321 (206)

MOTION TO QUASH SUBPOENAS
Off Calendar May-16-2005 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
NO APPEARANCES. TENTATIVE RULING ADOPTED. Plaintiff's Motion To Quash Defendant Foster Wheeler's Subpoenas, Or In The Alternative, Request For A Protective Order is ordered off calendar and the parties are ordered to meet and confer. The subpoenas are overly broad. The factual allegations made against the bankrupt entities, including witnesses and the amounts recovered from the bankrupt entities are discoverable. The demands by Plaintiff and any negotiations between Plaintiff and the entities are not discoverable. COMM: EVERETT A. HEWLETT
